tasks.subject: fix-streamdown-render-truncation [P1] Bump streamdown to ^2.4.0 and regenerate lockfile
tasks.description:
Update `apps/client/package.json` to pin `streamdown` to `^2.4.0` instead of `"latest"`, then run `pnpm install` from the repo root to regenerate the lockfile.

## Problem

`streamdown@2.3.0` bundles `remend@1.2.1`, which has a bug: when it encounters `[` inside an already-complete inline code span (e.g. `` `number[]` ``), it misidentifies it as the start of an incomplete link reference and discards everything from that `[` onward. This causes silent content truncation at streaming chunk boundaries. After hard reload the full text renders correctly because `remend` receives the complete string at once and handles it correctly.

`remend@1.2.2` (bundled in `streamdown@2.4.0`) fixes the root cause.

## Change 1: `apps/client/package.json` line 53

The file currently reads:
```json
"streamdown": "latest",
```

Change it to:
```json
"streamdown": "^2.4.0",
```

The `"latest"` specifier is an anti-pattern for production UI libraries: any future major or minor bump could silently introduce regressions on the next fresh install. `"^2.4.0"` (patch-compatible) is the preferred specifier — it receives patch fixes but not minor/major changes that could break the integration.

## Change 2: Regenerate lockfile

From the repo root, run:
```bash
pnpm install
```

This updates `pnpm-lock.yaml` to resolve `streamdown@2.4.0` and `remend@1.2.2` instead of `streamdown@2.3.0` and `remend@1.2.1`. No other dependencies are expected to change.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `apps/client/package.json` line 53 reads `"streamdown": "^2.4.0"` (not `"latest"`)
- [ ] `pnpm-lock.yaml` resolves `streamdown@2.4.0` and `remend@1.2.2`
- [ ] `pnpm install` completes without errors
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes (no TypeScript changes required)
- [ ] `pnpm test` suite passes (no behavioral changes to components)
tasks.activeForm: Bumping streamdown version and regenerating lockfile

tasks.subject: fix-streamdown-render-truncation [P1] Add regression test for array-type inline code truncation
